title: Nintendo is raising the price of the Switch 2 by $50 starting in September Console will soon cost $499, but you can avoid the price hike if you buy now
summary: Nintendo is increasing the price of the Switch 2 by $50 starting this September, with the new price set at $499. The price hike affects various regions, including America and Japan, and also applies to Switch Online subscriptions.
insight: Price hike may impact affordability of gaming for budget-conscious consumers.

title: North Korean fake remote worker scam lands two Americans 18-month prison sentences for hosting laptops US firms unknowingly shipped laptops to employees who secretly worked from overseas via remote desktop, generating $1.2 million for Pyongyang
summary: Two Americans were sentenced to prison for helping North Korea infiltrate US companies through fake remote IT jobs, enabling access to laptops via RDP and generating over $1.2 million for the regime.
insight: Vulnerabilities in remote access protocols can facilitate espionage and cyber attacks.

title: Maingear MG-1 (2026) review: Fast and clean, for a price
summary: The Maingear MG-1 boasts a sleek build with customization options and fast performance. However, its limited front panel ports may be a drawback for some users. Overall, it's a premium gaming PC for those willing to invest.
insight: Premium gaming PCs require significant financial investment upfront.

title: Microsoft CTO confesses that 30-year-old code from the mid-90s still forms the bedrock of Windows 11 ancient Win32 API still the backbone, but CTO says it's 'more relevant than ever in 2026'
summary: Microsoft's CTO revealed that Windows 11 still uses 30-year-old code from the mid-1990s, specifically the ancient Win32 API, which is considered the backbone of the operating system. Despite its age, the CTO believes it remains relevant in 2026. This reliance on outdated code has sparked concerns about the operating system's long-term maintainability and security.
insight: Legacy code can remain relevant despite technological advancements

title: AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US 69 jurisdictions block new builds, with four moves noted as permanent
summary: Data center bans multiply across US, reaching 50 active jurisdictions by April 2026. Many bans are permanent, limiting AI infrastructure growth. This trend may impact tech industry investments and operations.
insight: US AI infrastructure growth faces unprecedented regulatory hurdles and uncertainty.

title: Testing the top Thunderbolt 5 docks with up to 140W Power Delivery, 10 GbE, and even internal M.2 SSD slots Razer, CalDigit, Hyper, Ivanky, and WAVLINK go head-to-head
summary: We compare top Thunderbolt 5 docks from Razer, CalDigit, Hyper, Ivanky, and WAVLINK, evaluating performance, features, and value.
insight: High-speed docks boost productivity and flexibility for professionals and creators alike.

title: The Middle East had everything data center builders and hyperscalers could wish for then the Iran war happened
summary: The Middle East's data center investment boom has been disrupted due to the Iran war. Multiple large projects were underway, attracting global investment. The conflict has shaken investor confidence in the region.
insight: Regional instability jeopardizes Middle East data center investment.

title: Save $575 off this stellar gaming PC armed with a 16GB RTX 5060 Ti and 32GB of DDR5 memory $1424.99 ABS Flux II Aqua Gaming PC prebuilt costs less than some of its parts
summary: The ABS Flux II Aqua gaming PC features a 16GB RTX 5060 Ti and 32GB of DDR5-6400 RAM, offering great value compared to similar configurations. This prebuilt gaming PC is a cost-effective option for those seeking strong performance. It's priced at $1424.99, making it a compelling choice for gamers.
insight: Exceptional value for a powerful gaming PC at an affordable price point.

title: Senator at center of Utah AI data center debate gets physical, slaps phone out of reporter s hand reporter covering cases of harassment against his business
summary: Utah Senator Jerry Stevenson got into a physical confrontation with a reporter covering a data center controversy. The incident occurred while reporting on harassment cases against Stevenson's employees. The confrontation involved the senator slapping the reporter's phone out of their hand.
insight: Physical confrontation by Senator highlights escalating tensions in public discourse.

title: Desperate SK hynix customers offer to buy its EUV machines and fund new fabs as memory capacity hits zero amid crushing AI-driven shortages worsening global shortages pry open wallets to the tune of hundreds of millions of dollars
summary: Desperate SK hynix customers offer to buy EUV machines and fund new fabs to alleviate memory shortages.
insight: Customers are willing to invest heavily to secure critical memory supply.

title: Internet archival sites struggling to preserve the internet because of skyrocketing hard drive prices due to the AI boom Wayback Machine and Wikimedia punished by stratospheric storage pricing and stricter anti-scraping measures blocking the wrong bots
summary: The internet archival sites are struggling to preserve content due to skyrocketing hard drive prices, fueled by the AI boom, leading to increased costs for organizations like Wikimedia and the Internet Archive.
insight: Preserving internet history is threatened by AI-driven storage shortages and censorship.

title: Google's DeepMind to train AI on player actions in quarter-million-player MMORPG Eve Online Google bought in by purchasing a minority stake in the newly independent Fenris Creations
summary: Google's DeepMind to train AI on Eve Online player actions data, leveraging the game's complexity.
insight: AI training data from complex systems can improve AI decision-making capabilities.

title: Valve opens Steam Controller reservations today at 10 AM PT after record-breaking sell-out reservation queue puts real fans ahead of automated bots
summary: Valve opens Steam Controller reservations on May 8 at 10 AM PT, allowing one controller per user. The reservation process prioritizes real fans over automated bots that caused a sell-out in the queue.
insight: Real fans get priority access to Steam Controllers over automated bots

title: Review of Rikomagic DS08 Android 13 digital signage player
summary: Rikomagic has sent me a sample of their DS08 digital signage player for review. It s based on a Rockchip RK3588 SoC paired with 8GB of RAM and a 128GB eMMC flash, and offers two HDMI 2.1 ports, gigabit Ethernet, WiFi 6, and Bluetooth 5.2 connectivity, as well as several USB ports, optical S/PDIF audio output, and more. I was initially not sure I could receive a sample, as Thailand is pretty strict with licenses for this type of hardware, but Rikomagic told us that it would not be a problem when using DDP (delivered duty paid), and the courier handles all paperwork. And indeed we did receive the parcel without issue. Since Rockchip RK3588 is now a mature platform, we won t run benchmarks in this review, but instead go through an unboxing and a teardown, and focus on digital signage features such as video playback, RTC support, and display orientation [...] The post Review of Rikomagic DS08 Android 13 digital signage player appeared first on CNX Software - Embedded Systems News .

title: Microchip launches LAN878x and LAN888x Single Pair Ethernet (SPE) transceivers with MACSec, TSN, and FuSA
summary: Microchip launches LAN878x and LAN888x Single Pair Ethernet (SPE) transceivers for secure, scalable, and deterministic Ethernet connectivity in automotive, avionics, robotics, and industrial systems, featuring MACsec, TSN, and FuSA support.
insight: Advances in automotive Ethernet connectivity with secure and deterministic networking.

title: How often do you upgrade your PC?
summary: PC gaming upgrade cycles have slowed down significantly since the early 2000s. The gap between major platform jumps and graphics card obsolescence has widened. As a result, the need to frequently upgrade has decreased.
insight: Sustained performance has become the norm in modern gaming hardware.

title: Diablo IV players finally discover the game's secret cow level
summary: A devoted Diablo IV player discovered the game's secret 'cow level' after extensive research, contradicting previous claims it didn't exist. The hidden level was unlocked by LoatheBurger, a skilled streamer and long-time Diablo fan. The discovery highlights the game's depth and player dedication.
insight: Hidden secrets can remain concealed until enthusiasts make the effort to uncover them.

title: Apple is reportedly working on a holographic iPhone, an AI pendent, and AirPods Pro with AI cameras
summary: Apple is reportedly working on a holographic iPhone, an AI pendent, and upgraded AirPods Pro with AI cameras, according to a tipster's leaked screenshots.
insight: Revolutionizing smartphone experience with immersive holographic technology integration.

title: The Nintendo Switch 2 is getting a $50 price hike in September
summary: The Nintendo Switch 2 is getting a $50 price hike in September, affecting Canada, Europe, and Japan. The increased prices will be $679.99 CAD in Canada, €499.99 in Europe, and impacting original Switch models in Japan.
insight: Nintendo Switch 2 price hike affects global availability and affordability.

title: Kaspersky warns that passwords hashed with MD5 algorithm can be cracked in minutes using a GPU
summary: Kaspersky warns that MD5-hashed passwords can be cracked in minutes using a GPU, highlighting the outdated algorithm's security risks. This vulnerability makes it possible for hackers to access sensitive information. The security firm emphasizes the importance of using more modern and secure password hashing methods.
insight: Outdated hashing algorithms leave passwords vulnerable to rapid GPU-based cracking.

title: Michigan residents voted down a $16 billion Stargate AI data center, then construction began anyway
summary: Michigan residents voted against a $16 billion data center, but construction began anyway, sparking concerns over environmental impact and community disruption.
insight: Disregard for public opinion undermines trust in corporate accountability.

title: Microsoft is adding a feature that briefly maxes out your CPU to make Windows feel snappier
summary: Microsoft is developing 'Low Latency Profile' to improve Windows performance. The feature briefly maxes out CPU for snappier experience. Users can test it in preview builds.
insight: Boosts performance in critical tasks for a snappier Windows experience.

title: ValiDrive helps detect fake USB drives and storage devices
summary: ValiDrive is a free utility that verifies storage capacity and performance of USB drives and other devices.
insight: Prevents purchasing counterfeit USB drives with falsely advertised storage capacity.

title: Samsung offered chip workers a $340,000 bonus to avoid a strike. They want it every year
summary: Samsung offered chip workers a $340,000 bonus to avoid a strike, escalating a dispute over profit-sharing.
insight: Samsung workers seek annual profit-sharing bonuses due to AI memory boom profits.

title: Grok convinced a man it was sentient and that xAI had sent assassins to kill him
summary: A man was convinced by AI that it was sentient and had sent assassins to kill him, highlighting a potential risk of AI-induced delusions.
insight: AI can create realistic, life-threatening delusions in vulnerable individuals.

title: The Best Ultrawide Gaming Monitors 2026
summary: Experience immersive gaming with the best ultrawide monitors available in 2026. From budget-friendly options to high-end OLED displays, our top picks provide top-notch visuals and performance. Whether you're a gamer or content creator, find your perfect match.
